title: styphelia hispida
intro: Styphelia hispida, also known as the prickly styphelia, is a type of flowering plant native to Australia and New Zealand. It is a shrub-like plant with small white or pink flowers, and has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Styphelia hispida include relief from skin irritations and wounds, as well as potential anti-cancer properties. Herbal preparations of Styphelia hispida can be made into teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
